items.title: Zero units for 97110
items.detail: Therapeutic Exercise has 3s recorded but 0 billable units under the 8-minute rule.

items.title: Establish Right Shoulder Baseline ROM
items.detail:
Document specific active and passive range of motion measurements (in degrees) for the right shoulder before beginning therapeutic exercise—this establishes medical necessity and provides a baseline for progress tracking.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

items.title: Capture Shoulder Pain Characteristics
items.detail:
Record the patient's pain on a 0–10 scale and describe its character (sharp, dull, aching, throbbing) and what movements or activities aggravate or relieve it.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

items.title: Document Exercise Specifics During Treatment
items.detail:
As you perform therapeutic exercise, note the specific technique, number of sets/reps, resistance level, and patient response (tolerance, any symptom change).

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

audit_log.detail: Session finalized for region US via bedrock

latest_analysis.summary: Conversation segment reviewed: therapeutic exercise for shoulder pain
possible_clinical_impressions: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation
possible_diagnoses: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation

cpt_suggestions.label: therapeutic_exercise
cpt_suggestions.descriptor: Application of therapeutic procedures to one or more areas, each 15 minutes, utilizing structured exercises designed to improve, manage, or restore physiological range of motion, physical strength, muscle endurance, and soft tissue flexibility.
documentation_requirements: Objective baseline metrics for strength (e.g., manual muscle testing grades) and range of motion measured via goniometry.
Clear descriptions of specific exercises performed, including sets, repetitions, resistance levels, and anatomical areas targeted.
Explicit documentation of skilled clinical intervention, such as verbal cues for form correction, manual assistance, or physiological monitoring.
Direct correlation between the selected exercises and the functional progression goals outlined in the patient care plan.

billing_caveats.time_system: CMS 8-Minute Rule compliant. Total cumulative timed minutes across all direct treatments determine total billable units per session.

payer_specific_rules.medicare_part_b: Requires direct, one-on-one visual and manual contact by a licensed clinician or certified assistant.
payer_specific_rules.commercial_payers: Some non-Medicare lines follow the Substantial Portion Method (15-minute midpoint rules) rather than cumulative rounding.

ncci_conflict_risks: Bundled with 97140 (Manual Therapy) when executed on identical anatomical zones unless distinct time intervals and separate goals are validated.
Subject to overutilization audits if billed consistently as the sole intervention across multi-week cycles without functional progression.

cpt_suggestions.reason: Indicated when a patient presents with demonstrable deficits in joint mobility, active or passive range of motion, muscular weakness, or diminished structural endurance resulting from injury, surgery, or chronic degenerative disease processes.

soap_update.assessment: Possible clinical impressions to review: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation.
soap_update.plan: Review therapy plan, skilled minutes, and documentation support for detected treatment themes. Documentation gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.; Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise..

billing_hints: Documented 'therapeutic exercise' may support Therapeutic Exercise (97110).
latest_analysis.disclaimer: Rules-based detection requires clinician review before billing.

subjective.pain_scale: Not documented in session. Recommend capturing 0-10 pain scale rating and character (sharp, dull, aching) with aggravating/relieving factors at next visit.
subjective.duration: Not documented in session. Recommend establishing onset, frequency, and duration of symptoms.

objective.observation_notes: Therapeutic exercise performed for right shoulder. Patient engaged in treatment. Specific exercise parameters (sets, reps, resistance level, technique details) and patient tolerance/response require documentation at next visit per compliance recommendations.
objective.range_of_motion: Baseline active and passive range of motion measurements for right shoulder not documented in session. Recommend establishing specific ROM measurements (in degrees) for shoulder flexion, abduction, and internal/external rotation to establish medical necessity and track progress.
objective.affect: Patient participated in therapeutic exercise session.

assessment.diagnosis_summary: Patient presented with right shoulder pain and received therapeutic exercise intervention. Session duration and specific exercise parameters were limited in documentation. Baseline manual muscle testing and range of motion measurements were not established prior to exercise, limiting ability to quantify functional baseline and medical necessity. Recommend comprehensive baseline assessment including ROM, MMT, and pain characterization at next visit to support continued skilled therapy and track objective progress toward functional goals. Documentation gaps to address: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise..
assessment.primary_diagnosis_code: Not documented in session. Recommend establishing primary ICD-10 diagnosis code (e.g., M25.511 for right shoulder pain) for medical necessity and billing accuracy.

plan.follow_up_plan: Continue therapeutic exercise for right shoulder. Recommend establishing at next visit: (1) baseline active/passive ROM measurements in degrees; (2) baseline manual muscle testing grades for shoulder musculature; (3) pain scale rating (0-10) with character and aggravating/relieving factors; (4) specific exercise prescription with sets, reps, and resistance level; (5) home exercise program with written instructions; (6) activity modification guidance; (7) patient education on symptom management. Establish visit frequency and duration based on functional goals and medical necessity. Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise.

compliance_gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.
Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise.

patient_summary.summary:
Hi Jane! Today we worked on therapeutic exercises to help with your shoulder pain. We started building a foundation for your recovery, and we want to make sure we're tracking your progress carefully. At your next visit, we'll measure your shoulder movement more precisely and establish a specific exercise plan tailored to your needs. Keep up with any exercises we've discussed, and we look forward to seeing you continue to improve!

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
